SEARCH
Package de.jvstvshd.necrify.common.user

Class AbstractConsoleUser

java.lang.Object
de.jvstvshd.necrify.common.user.AbstractConsoleUser
All Implemented Interfaces:
CommandSender, NecrifyUser
Direct Known Subclasses:
VelocityConsoleUser
public abstract class AbstractConsoleUser extends Object implements NecrifyUser

  • Method Details

    • getUuid

      @NotNull public @NotNull UUID getUuid()
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      The UUID of the user. This UUID is the same as the one used by Mojang and is unique to each user.
      Specified by:
      getUuid in interface NecrifyUser
      Returns:
      the UUID of the user.

    • getUsername

      @Nullable public @Nullable String getUsername()
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      The username of the user. This is the username of the user's Minecraft account. This method may return null if the name is unknown to the system since only a UUID is required to identify a user. If a non-null value is returned, it is only the last-known username. The current username associated with the UUID may differ.
      Specified by:
      getUsername in interface NecrifyUser
      Returns:
      the last-known username of the user

    • ban

      @NotNull public @NotNull CompletableFuture<Ban> ban(@Nullable @Nullable net.kyori.adventure.text.Component reason, @NotNull @NotNull PunishmentDuration duration)
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      Bans the user with the given reason and duration. A banned user is not able to join the server this system belongs to while the ban is active. The duration is the time the ban is active. For permanent bans you may also refer to NecrifyUser.banPermanent(Component). If this user is still online, he will be removed from the server immediately after the ban was enforced. The Punishment.punish() method is called before the object instance is returned. An additional, manual call to this method is not required and may cause data inconsistency.
      Specified by:
      ban in interface NecrifyUser
      Parameters:
      reason - The reason why the user is banned. This reason is displayed to the user when they try to join the server. If null, a configurable default reason is used.
      duration - How long the ban is active.
      Returns:
      the ban object representing the ban. The ban may not be active yet, as the execution takes some time to complete.

    • banPermanent

      @NotNull public @NotNull CompletableFuture<Ban> banPermanent(@Nullable @Nullable net.kyori.adventure.text.Component reason)
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      Bans the user permanently with the given reason. A permanently banned user is not able to join the server this system belongs to ever again. If this user is still online, he will be removed from the server immediately after the ban was enforced. The Punishment.punish() method is called before the object instance is returned. An additional, manual call to this method is not required and may cause data inconsistency.
      Specified by:
      banPermanent in interface NecrifyUser
      Parameters:
      reason - The reason why the user is banned. This reason is displayed to the user when they try to join the server. If null, a configurable default reason is used.
      Returns:
      the ban object representing the ban. The ban may not be active yet, as the execution takes some time to complete.

    • mute

      @NotNull public @NotNull CompletableFuture<Mute> mute(@Nullable @Nullable net.kyori.adventure.text.Component reason, @NotNull @NotNull PunishmentDuration duration)
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      Mutes the user with the given reason and duration. A muted user is not able to send messages in the chat of the server this system belongs to while the mute is active. The duration is the time the mute is active. For permanent mutes, you may also refer to NecrifyUser.mutePermanent(Component). The Punishment.punish() method is called before the object instance is returned. An additional, manual call to this method is not required and may cause data inconsistency.
      Specified by:
      mute in interface NecrifyUser
      Parameters:
      reason - The reason why the user is muted. This reason is displayed to the user when they try to send a message. If null, a configurable default reason is used.
      duration - How long the mute is active.
      Returns:
      the mute object representing the mute. The mute may not be active yet, as the execution takes some time to complete.

    • mutePermanent

      @NotNull public @NotNull CompletableFuture<Mute> mutePermanent(@Nullable @Nullable net.kyori.adventure.text.Component reason)
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      Mutes the user permanently with the given reason. A permanently muted user is not able to send messages in the chat of the server this system belongs to ever again. The Punishment.punish() method is called before the object instance is returned. An additional, manual call to this method is not required and may cause data inconsistency.
      Specified by:
      mutePermanent in interface NecrifyUser
      Parameters:
      reason - The reason why the user is muted. This reason is displayed to the user when they try to send a message. If null, a configurable default reason is used.
      Returns:
      the mute object representing the mute. The mute may not be active yet, as the execution takes some time to complete.

    • kick

      @NotNull public @NotNull CompletableFuture<Kick> kick(@Nullable @Nullable net.kyori.adventure.text.Component reason)
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      Kicks the user with the given reason. A kicked user is removed from the server this system belongs to. They are able to join again immediately after being kicked if not banned. The reason is displayed to the user when they are kicked.
      Specified by:
      kick in interface NecrifyUser
      Parameters:
      reason - The reason why the user is kicked. This reason is displayed to the user when they are kicked. If null, a configurable default reason is used.
      Returns:
      the kick object representing the kick. The kick may not be active yet, as the execution takes some time to complete.

    • getPunishments

      @NotNull public <T extends Punishment> @NotNull List<T> getPunishments(PunishmentType... types)
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      This method queries all punishments with the given UUID of a player and returns them in a list. All punishments that are returned are still running, i.e. they are not expired or revoked.
      Specified by:
      getPunishments in interface NecrifyUser
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of punishment(s), matching them in type
      Parameters:
      types - the types of punishments to query. If no type is given, all punishments that are stored as running punishments are queried (not including one-time punishments like kicks).
      Returns:
      the list of punishments which are stored at the moment. This list may contains punishments which are over.

    • queryUsername

      @NotNull public @NotNull CompletableFuture<String> queryUsername(boolean update)
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      Queries the account username through the Mojang API. This method may take some time to complete. You may also update the user name by setting the update parameter to true. If the username is updated, it is also returned. If not, only
      Specified by:
      queryUsername in interface NecrifyUser
      Parameters:
      update - Whether the username should be updated. If true, the username is updated and returned. If false, it will only be returned.
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing the username of the user. This future may be completed exceptionally if the username could not be queried.

    • isWhitelisted

      public boolean isWhitelisted()
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      Checks whether the user is whitelisted. If the user is whitelisted and the server whitelist is active, they are able to join the server this system belongs to.
      Specified by:
      isWhitelisted in interface NecrifyUser
      Returns:
      true if the user is whitelisted, false otherwise.

    • setWhitelisted

      public CompletableFuture<Boolean> setWhitelisted(boolean whitelisted)
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      Sets the user to be whitelisted. If the user is whitelisted and the server whitelist is active, they are able to join the server this system belongs to.

      The user also gets kicked if they are online and get blacklisted if the whitelist is active.

      Specified by:
      setWhitelisted in interface NecrifyUser
      Parameters:
      whitelisted - true if the user is whitelisted, false otherwise.
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing the result of the operation. This future completes with true if anything has changed and false if the whitelist status is already set to the given value.

    • delete

      public CompletableFuture<Integer> delete(@NotNull @NotNull UserDeletionReason reason)
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      Deletes this user from the system. This will also delete all punishments associated with this user. This action is irreversible and should be used with caution.

      The reason for the deletion is required and is used to log the deletion reason and inform listeners about the deletion and its reasoning.

      Specified by:
      delete in interface NecrifyUser
      Parameters:
      reason - the reason for the deletion.
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture containing the amount of affected rows and punishments.

    • hasPermission

      public boolean hasPermission(@NotNull @NotNull String permission)
      Description copied from interface: CommandSender
      Checks whether the command sender has a certain permission. This requires a permission system to be set up and the entity in question to exist.
      Specified by:
      hasPermission in interface CommandSender
      Parameters:
      permission - a non-null string that represents the permission to be checked.
      Returns:
      true if the command sender has the permission, false otherwise.

    • getLocale

      @NotNull public @NotNull Locale getLocale()
      Description copied from interface: NecrifyUser
      Gets this player's current locale. This locale is used to provide messages in the user's language. If the locale is not set, the default locale is used. Note: The locale may be provided from the language the user has set in their Minecraft client. This information is only sent after the client connected to the server, so calls to this during the connection process usually return the default locale.
      Specified by:
      getLocale in interface NecrifyUser
      Returns:
      the locale of the user.

    • sendMessage

      public void sendMessage(@NotNull @NotNull String key, net.kyori.adventure.text.format.TextColor color, net.kyori.adventure.text.Component... args)
      Description copied from interface: CommandSender
      Sends a message to the command sender. The message must contain a valid translation key that is present in the language files of the server. The message will be translated to the correct language when being displayed, if a translation is available. If this key does not map to a translation, the key itself will be displayed. The base message (excluding any placeholders) will be colored with the given color.

      Per default, this will use MessageProvider.provide(String, Component...)

      Specified by:
      sendMessage in interface CommandSender
      Parameters:
      key - a non-null string that represents the translation key of the message to be sent.
      color - the color to be used for the base message.
      args - the arguments to be passed to the translation to fill placeholders.

    • provider

      public MessageProvider provider()
